A1-QUIZ.title: RFI Fundamentals
A1-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of RFI foundations

questions.stem: What does RFI mean in preflop poker?
questions.explanation: RFI stands for Raise First In — the first voluntary raise preflop.

questions.stem: Which position should generally use the tightest opening range?
questions.explanation: UTG opens tightest because more players remain and position is worst.

questions.stem: Why can players open a wider range from late position?

choices.label: They are forced to show their cards less often
They act later postflop and realize equity better
The blinds are not allowed to defend
Late position hands are worth more automatically

questions.explanation: Late position lets you act last postflop, improving equity realization.

questions.stem: What is a strong default lesson takeaway for RFI sizing?

choices.label: Use a consistent default size rather than changing size by hand strength
Always raise bigger from the button than UTG
Use random sizes to stay unreadable

questions.explanation: Default to a consistent open size to avoid giving away hand strength.

A2-QUIZ.title: 3-Bet Strategy Basics
A2-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of core 3-bet concepts

questions.stem: What is a 3-bet preflop?

choices.label: The first raise in the pot
A re-raise versus an open raise

questions.explanation: A 3-bet is the re-raise after an initial open.

questions.stem: Which pair best describes the two main reasons to 3-bet?
questions.explanation: 3-bets are primarily for value or as pressure/bluff.

questions.stem: A linear 3-bet range is usually made up of:

choices.label: Premium hands plus strong playable hands

questions.explanation: Linear ranges stack strong value hands and strong playable combos.

questions.stem: What is the standard sizing idea for a 3-bet out of position?

choices.label: Always the same as the open size

questions.explanation: Out of position 3-bets are typically larger to offset disadvantage.

A3-QUIZ.title: Defending vs 3-Bets
A3-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of defending against 3-bets

questions.stem: When you face a 3-bet after opening, what are your three basic options?

choices.label: Limp, call, or jam every time
Fold, raise, or show one card

questions.explanation: Versus a 3-bet you can fold, call, or 4-bet.

questions.stem: Which type of hand is usually a better call versus a 3-bet in position?
questions.explanation: Suited, connected hands realize equity better in position.

questions.stem: Why are dominated offsuit broadways often poor calls versus a 3-bet?

choices.label: They are too strong to call and must always 4-bet
They realize equity poorly and are often dominated
They can only win by making a straight
They block your opponent from bluffing

questions.explanation: Dominated offsuit broadways suffer from reverse implied odds.

questions.stem: What is the best default positional adjustment when facing a 3-bet?

choices.label: Continue much wider out of position
Ignore position and play your exact chart only
Call wider in position and tighten out of position

questions.explanation: Position drives defense: call wider IP, tighten OOP.

A4-QUIZ.title: Blind Defense Fundamentals
A4-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of blind defense

questions.stem: Why is big blind defense important?

choices.label: Because the big blind always has the best odds to 3-bet
Because folding too much lets open-raisers profit too easily
Because the big blind is always in position postflop
Because suited hands are mandatory calls

questions.explanation: Overfolding the big blind lets opponents profit automatically.

questions.stem: What does MDF stand for in poker strategy?
questions.explanation: MDF = minimum defense frequency.

questions.stem: In multiway pots from the big blind, what is a common mistake?

choices.label: Defending more weak offsuit hands just because pot odds look attractive

questions.explanation: Pot odds alone can trick you into over-defending weak offsuit hands.

questions.stem: Which hands usually realize equity better as blind defenses?

choices.label: Any two broadways, regardless of domination

questions.explanation: Suited, connected hands play better postflop and realize equity.

A5-QUIZ.title: Isolation Raising
A5-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of ISO raising concepts

questions.stem: What is the main goal of an isolation raise after a player limps?

choices.label: Keep the pot as small as possible
Force the hand to go all-in preflop
Play a bigger pot against the weaker limper, ideally heads-up
Invite the blinds in with better odds

questions.explanation: ISO raises aim to isolate the limper and play heads-up.

questions.stem: Which rule of thumb best matches a standard ISO sizing baseline?
questions.explanation: A common ISO sizing baseline is 3bb + 1bb per limper.

questions.stem: Compared with an RFI range, a solid ISO range is usually:

choices.label: Only pocket pairs and suited aces

questions.explanation: ISO ranges are often a bit tighter than standard RFIs.

questions.stem: What usually happens when your ISO raise size is too small?

choices.label: You make your hand look stronger
You invite multiple callers and fail to isolate effectively

questions.explanation: Too-small ISO sizing invites callers and fails to isolate.

A6-QUIZ.title: Stack Depth Adjustments
A6-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of stack depth strategy

questions.stem: Why does stack depth matter preflop?

choices.label: It changes hand values, range construction, and postflop plans
It matters only in cash games, never tournaments
It does not affect opening ranges

questions.explanation: Stack depth impacts value, ranges, and postflop planning.

questions.stem: Which hand class generally gains value when stacks are deep?
questions.explanation: Deep stacks increase implied odds for small pairs and suited connectors.

questions.stem: With shallow stacks, which type of hands should you prioritize more often?

choices.label: Hands that rely heavily on implied odds only
Broadway cards and pocket pairs that make top pair or strong equity
Weak suited trash because stacks are smaller
Hands that need four streets of bluffing room

questions.explanation: Shallow stacks favor hands that make immediate, strong value.

questions.stem: What happens to implied odds when stacks get shorter?

choices.label: They shrink, so speculative hands lose some value
They only matter in blind versus blind pots

questions.explanation: Short stacks reduce implied odds, hurting speculative hands.

A7-QUIZ.title: 30-Day Preflop Improvement Plan
A7-QUIZ.description: Test your understanding of the 30-day preflop plan

questions.stem: What is the main point of a structured 30-day preflop plan?

choices.label: Memorize every chart in one sitting
Build skill through focused, repeatable practice over time
Replace all hand history study with guessing

questions.explanation: A structured plan builds habits through consistent practice.

questions.stem: Which daily routine best matches the lesson's 10-minute training model?

choices.label: Study one spot, drill hands, then review mistakes
Watch highlights only and skip practice
Play random cash games without feedback
Memorize charts for ten minutes without testing

questions.explanation: The routine is focused study, drills, then review.

questions.stem: What should a useful practice scorecard include?

choices.label: Favorite poker emojis and table colors
Accuracy percentage, recurring mistakes, and one rule for next session
Only total hands played over a lifetime

questions.explanation: A scorecard tracks accuracy, mistakes, and a next-step rule.

questions.stem: Why is focused practice better than trying to fix everything at once?

choices.label: Because purposeful practice works best with one specific spot and feedback
Because broad study makes charts illegal to use
Because poker rewards randomness over discipline

questions.explanation: Focused practice lets you target one spot with feedback.

D1.title: RFI Decision Lab
D1.description: Open or fold decisions from multiple positions.

questions.stem: UTG: A♠ Q♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Strong broadway is a default open from UTG.

questions.stem: UTG: A♦ 9♠ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Offsuit weak ace is dominated too often from EP.

questions.stem: HJ: K♦ T♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Suited broadway is playable in HJ, but fold to 3-bets if pressured.

questions.stem: HJ: Q♠ T♣ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Offsuit QTo is too dominated in earlier positions.

questions.stem: CO: A♠ 4♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Weak offsuit aces are dominated and reverse-implied in late position.

questions.stem: BTN: J♦ T♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Strong suited broadway is a clear button open.

questions.stem: SB (first-in): A♦ Q♣ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Strong AQ is still an open from the small blind.

questions.stem: BTN: 9♦ 5♠ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Offsuit trash still under-realizes even on the button.

D2.title: 3-Bet or Defend Lab
D2.description: Choose between 3-bet, call, or fold versus opens.

questions.stem: BTN vs CO open: A♠ Q♠ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AQ suited is a value 3-bet against a CO open.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: K♦ J♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: KJ suited defends well in the BB; mixing call is standard.

questions.stem: SB vs BTN open: A♥ 5♥ — Best action?
questions.explanation: In the SB, prefer 3-bet or fold rather than flatting.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: Q♠ J♠ — Best action?
questions.explanation: QJ suited plays well as a defend without inflating the pot.

questions.stem: BTN vs HJ open: 8♠ 8♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Pairs are fine calls in position; 3-bet sometimes but call is standard.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: A♦ 9♣ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Weak offsuit aces are dominated and should be folded in the BB.

questions.stem: SB vs CO open: K♣ Q♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Strong broadway is a value 3-bet from the SB.

questions.stem: BTN vs UTG open: A♣ J♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AJ offsuit is often dominated against a tight UTG range.

D3.title: BB Defense Speed Drill
D3.description: Rapid-fire BB defense decisions. Answer fast and move on.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: K♠ Q♠ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Strong suited broadway is a value 3-bet versus BTN.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: Q♦ J♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: QJ suited is a solid call in the BB versus BTN.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: A♣ 8♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Weak offsuit aces are dominated even with pot odds.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: 9♣ 8♣ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Suited connectors are defendable versus BTN.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: A♠ Q♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AQ is a clear value 3-bet versus CO.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: K♦ T♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: KTs is a defendable call versus CO.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: J♠ 7♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Dominated offsuit broadways should be folded versus CO.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: 7♦ 2♣ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Trash offsuit hands still fold even with pot odds.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: A♦ J♣ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AJ is strong enough to 3-bet for value versus BTN.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: 6♠ 6♥ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Small pairs are standard calls for set value.

D4.title: Final Assessment
D4.description: Cumulative preflop assessment across positions and pressure spots.

questions.stem: UTG: K♠ Q♠ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: KQs is a solid UTG open in the baseline.

questions.stem: UTG: A♦ 8♠ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Offsuit weak aces should fold from early position.

questions.stem: BTN vs CO open: A♣ Q♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AQ is a value 3-bet against CO.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: J♠ 9♠ — Best action?
questions.explanation: J9 suited is a defendable BB call.

questions.stem: SB vs BTN open: A♠ K♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AK is a premium value 3-bet from the SB.

questions.stem: HJ: 9♣ 8♣ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Suited connectors can open from HJ in the baseline.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: A♠ Q♥ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AQ is a value 3-bet versus CO.

questions.stem: BTN vs UTG open: A♣ J♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: AJ offsuit is often dominated against UTG.

questions.stem: SB first-in: Q♦ 9♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: SB ranges stay tighter; Q9 suited is a fold in the baseline.

questions.stem: BTN: J♦ T♦ — Open or Fold?
questions.explanation: Strong suited broadways open on the button.

questions.stem: BB vs BTN open: A♣ 2♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Weak offsuit aces are folds in the BB.

questions.stem: BB vs CO open: J♦ 7♦ — Best action?
questions.explanation: Dominated offsuit broadways should be folded versus CO.
